lede: A look at June 15 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Tim Lincecum and Hugo Pratt, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: The Troubles
events.desc: The Troubles: The Provisional Irish Republican Army (IRA) detonates a powerful truck bomb in the middle of Manchester, England, devastating the city centre and injuring 200 people.

events.title: Supreme Court of the United States
events.desc: The United States Supreme Court rules in United States v. Álvarez-Machaín that it is permissible for the United States to forcibly extradite suspects in foreign countries and bring them to the United States for trial, without approval from those other countries.

events.title: Mount Pinatubo
events.desc: In the Philippines, Mount Pinatubo erupts in the second largest volcanic eruption of the 20th century, killing over 800 people.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Operation Aerial begins: Allied troops start to evacuate France, following Germany's takeover of Paris and most of the nation.

events.title: 1896 Sanriku earthquake
events.desc: One of the deadliest tsunamis in Japan's history kills more than 22,000 people.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: The Second Battle of Petersburg begins.

events.title: New Hampshire
events.desc: New Hampshire approves the Twelfth Amendment to the United States Constitution, ratifying the document.

events.title: Pope Leo X
events.desc: Pope Leo X threatens to excommunicate Martin Luther in Exsurge Domine.

events.title: Byzantine Empire
events.desc: The Byzantine Empire and the Republic of Venice conclude a treaty. Apart from agreeng on a ten year truce, the Venetians are alloted a commercial quarter in Constantinople and are restored to earlier privileges.

events.title: John, King of England
events.desc: King John of England puts his seal to Magna Carta.

births.name: Xi Jinping
births.line: Chinese engineer and politician, General Secretary of the Communist Party and President of China

deaths.text: James K. Polk, American lawyer and politician, 11th President of the United States

observances.text: Christian feast day: Abraham of Clermont (or of St Cyriacus)
Christian feast day: Alice (or Adelaide) of Schaerbeek
Christian feast day: Evelyn Underhill (Church of England and The Episcopal Church)
